changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the CCCS CM-2 control by focusing on maintaining a secure baseline configuration in Kubernetes. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but the audit logging function needed a more specific name to align with the API server context. All functions are technically accurate and relevant to Kubernetes security controls.
naming_improvements: Ensure audit logging functions specify the component they relate to, such as 'apiserver'.
title: CCCS CM-2: Baseline Configuration
step1_reasoning: The CCCS CM-2 control focuses on maintaining a baseline configuration for security, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring least privilege access through RBAC, securing network traffic with NetworkPolicies, encrypting data at rest in etcd, and enabling audit logging to monitor and maintain the baseline configuration. These checks ensure that the Kubernetes cluster maintains a secure and compliant baseline configuration.
